notes.text: ➕ All About Adding & Subtracting ➖

Q: What is Addition? A: Addition is like a math magic trick where we put things together to get a bigger amount! If you have 2 apples 🍎🍎 and your friend gives you 3 more 🍎🍎🍎, you add them to get 5 apples in total! The numbers we put together are called addends, and the final answer is called the sum.
Q: What is an Addition Statement? A: It's a math sentence that tells the story of our adding magic. Example: 4 + 3 = 7. The ➕ symbol means add!

Q: What is Subtraction? A: Subtraction is when we take things away! If you have 4 cookies 🍪🍪🍪🍪 and you eat 1 cookie 🍪, you subtract it, and you have 3 cookies left! The big number we start with is the minuend, the number we take away is the subtrahend, and what is left over is the difference. The ➖ symbol means take away!

Q: What is a Number Bond? A: Number bonds are two number best friends that hold hands to make a new number! 🤝 Example: For the number 5, the best friends could be 4 and 1 (4 + 1 = 5), or 2 and 3 (2 + 3 = 5).
📖 Numbers & Stories 🔢
Q: What is a Word Problem or Story Problem? A: It is a fun mini-story that has a secret math puzzle hidden inside for you to solve! It helps us use math in real life.
Q: What is a Times Table? A: A times table is a cool shortcut for counting! It is a list that shows what happens when we add the exact same number over and over again.

Q: What is the difference between Cardinal and Ordinal numbers? A: Cardinal Numbers are for counting how many things we have (1, 2, 3 cars 🚗🚗🚗). Ordinal Numbers tell us who won the race or what position something is in! (1st place 🥇, 2nd place 🥈, 3rd place 🥉).
📏 Measuring & Sorting 📊
Q: What is Measurement? A: It is how we find out how tall, heavy, or big something is! We can measure how long a pencil is ✏️, how heavy a watermelon is 🍉, or how hot the sun is ☀️.
Q: What is Data Handling? A: It is like sorting your toy box! We collect information and organize it into neat tables or charts so we can easily read it and see exactly what we have.
Q: What is a Pattern? A: A pattern is a special rule where things repeat in the exact same order! Example: Apple, banana, apple, banana 🍎🍌🍎🍌.
🔺 Shapes All Around Us 🟩
Q: What is a Shape? A: A shape is the outline or outer boundary of an object. It tells us what something looks like, no matter what color or size it is!
Q: What is a Plane Shape? A: A plane shape is totally flat, like a drawing on a piece of paper. It has no thickness! Examples: A Triangle 🔺, a Square 🟩, a Rectangle 🟨, or a Circle 🔵.
Q: What is a Solid Shape? A: A solid shape is puffy and takes up real space! You can hold it in your hands. Examples: A bouncy ball (Sphere ⚽), a toy block (Cube 🎲), or a delivery box (Cuboid 📦).
⚖️ Comparing Numbers 🎢

Q: What is Comparison? A: It is looking at two numbers to see which is bigger, smaller, or if they are the exact same! We use hungry alligator mouths to show this: > (greater than) and < (less than). The alligator always opens its mouth to eat the bigger number! 🐊 We use = (equal to) if the numbers are exactly the same.

Q: What do Ascending and Descending Order mean? A: Ascending Order means Going Up! 🧗‍♂️ You arrange numbers from the smallest to the biggest, like climbing up the stairs: 1, 2, 3, 4. Descending Order means Going Down! 🎢 You arrange numbers from the biggest to the smallest, like sliding down a slide: 4, 3, 2, 1.

questions.story: 📖 Story Time! Sita and Rita are making yummy triangular sandwiches. Look at the picture — you can count them in two groups!

image.alt: Six triangular sandwiches arranged in two columns

questions.q: Sita made 3 sandwiches. Rita made 3 more sandwiches. When we put groups together, we ADD! How many sandwiches are there in all?
questions.hint: Addition is putting things together! 3 and 3 are the addends. 3 + 3 = 6, so 6 is the sum. This is a story problem — a mini-story with a math puzzle inside! 🍞

questions.story: 🤝 Number Bond Magic! Number bonds are two number best friends that hold hands to make a bigger number. The big circle is the whole, and the two small circles are the parts.

image.alt: Number bond diagram with 8 in the big circle

questions.q: Look at the picture. The whole number in the big circle is 8. Which pair of best-friend numbers adds up to make 8?
questions.hint: Yes! 5 and 3 are number bonds for 8 because 5 + 3 = 8. You can also use 4 + 4, 6 + 2, or 7 + 1 — any two parts that make the whole! 🤝

options: Putting things together to get a bigger amount
questions.answer: Putting things together to get a bigger amount

questions.q: In 2 + 3 = 5, what is the answer 5 called?
What are the numbers we add together called?
questions.hint: They are the parts we put together.

questions.q: If you have 4 cookies and eat 1, what is left called?
Which pair is a number bond for 5?

questions.hint: They tell us position in a race!

questions.q: Counting how many cars you have uses…
A triangle drawn on paper is a…
A bouncy ball is an example of a…
questions.hint: You can hold it in your hands!

questions.q: The alligator mouth > always opens toward the…
questions.hint: The alligator is hungry for the bigger number! 🐊

questions.q: Arranging numbers 1, 2, 3, 4 from smallest to biggest is…
Apple, banana, apple, banana repeating is an example of a…
questions.hint: Things repeat in the same order!
